entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The crop species Zea mays L. whose germplasm is conserved in genebanks

entities.name: GENETIC INTEGRITY
entities.description: The maintenance of the original genetic composition of conserved germplasm

entities.name: SNP
entities.description: Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms used as molecular markers to assess genetic changes

entities.name: SNP HAPLOTYPE
entities.description: Combinations of SNP alleles used to analyze genetic diversity and integrity

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of genetic characteristics within the maize accessions

entities.name: ALLELE NUMBER
entities.description: The average number of alleles per locus used to measure genetic diversity

entities.name: SNP ALLELIC FREQUENCY
entities.description: The frequency of specific alleles at SNP loci used to detect genetic changes

entities.name: MAJOR ALLELIC FREQUENCY
entities.description: The frequency of the most common allele at a SNP locus

entities.name: POLYMORPHIC INFORMATION CONTENT
entities.description: A measure of the informativeness of SNP haplotype markers

entities.name: REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of growing out conserved seeds to maintain viability and genetic integrity

entities.name: GENEBANK
entities.description: Facilities where maize landrace accessions are conserved and regenerated

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKER
entities.description: Tools used to understand the extent of genetic integrity during regeneration
